questions.question: What is the primary function of the sliding window protocol in the data link layer?
options: A) To acknowledge received packets
B) To retransmit lost packets
C) To optimize network bandwidth
D) To manage congestion
questions.explanation: The sliding window protocol is designed to optimize network bandwidth by allowing the receiver to receive data without waiting for an acknowledgement from the sender for every packet.

questions.question: What is the key difference between the sliding window protocol and the stop-and-wait protocol?
options: A) The sender uses a larger window size
B) The receiver uses a larger window size
C) The sender must get acknowledgement for every packet
D) The receiver must get acknowledgement for every packet
questions.explanation: The key difference between the sliding window protocol and the stop-and-wait protocol is that the sliding window protocol allows the receiver to receive data without waiting for an acknowledgement from the sender for every packet, whereas the stop-and-wait protocol requires acknowledgement for every packet.

questions.question: What type of error can occur in the sliding window protocol?
options: A) Frame duplication
B) Frame loss
C) Frame corruption
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: The sliding window protocol is susceptible to frame loss, frame duplication, and frame corruption, which can cause retransmission errors.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the acknowledgement (ACK) packet in the sliding window protocol?
options: A) To retransmit lost packets
B) To verify the reception of packets
C) To manage congestion
D) To initiate the sliding window protocol
questions.explanation: The acknowledgement (ACK) packet in the sliding window protocol serves to verify the reception of packets by the receiver, allowing the sender to know whether the packets have been successfully received.

questions.question: What is the maximum size of the window in the sliding window protocol?
options: A) The maximum size of the window is equal to the packet size
B) The maximum size of the window is equal to the maximum transmission unit (MTU)
C) The maximum size of the window is determined by the receiver
D) The maximum size of the window is determined by the sender
questions.explanation: The maximum size of the window in the sliding window protocol is determined by the receiver, which typically sets the window size based on its available buffer space and the network bandwidth.
